message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: Catalan Mediterranean Cuisine (Rancho Mirage)
Cuisine/type signal: Spanish / Mediterranean
Why it surfaced in discovery: Locally owned independent on Highway 111; specializes in Spanish paella, charred octopus, Iberico charcuterie; consistently ranked among Rancho Mirage's top restaurants on TripAdvisor and Yelp
Discovery sources: https://catalanrestaurants.com/ | https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-g32942-d2646706-Reviews-Catalan_Mediterranean_Restaurant-Rancho_Mirage_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html | https://www.opentable.com/r/catalan-mediterranean-cuisine-rancho-mirage

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Catalan Mediterranean Cuisine in Rancho Mirage and their reviews:

## Restaurant Overview

Located in the heart of Rancho Mirage, Catalan Mediterranean Restaurant serves Spanish-infused dishes in a rustic yet modern setting, with Chef Drew Davis creating seasonal dishes, offering signature paella, homemade sangria, tapas plates and craft cocktails.

## Yelp Reviews

The restaurant has 999 reviews on Yelp as of June 2026, located at 70026 Hwy 111, Rancho Mirage, with hours 5:00 pm - 9:00 pm Monday through Friday and Saturday, and 5:00 pm - 8:00 pm on Sunday. The restaurant is offering a free house sangria with any entrée.

## TripAdvisor Reviews

Reviews on TripAdvisor show mixed feedback. Some positive reviews mention tapas style plates including octopus, Spanish spinach, mushroom plates, and jamon serrano with Spanish wine, with food being great and service being top notch. However, there are also negative reviews citing that the experience was "nothing special" and "too expensive" with staffing problems.

## Additional Positive Feedback

The restaurant has more than 300 positive reviews on Google Maps and is known for excellent service, perfect ambiance, and scrumptious food. The deep-water octopus is often described as "tender" and "perfectly charred," while the paella is praised as "really flavorful" and "a must-try."

# Catalan Mediterranean Cuisine - Business Profile

**Business Description:**
A locally owned, family-operated establishment describing themselves as "rooted right in the heart of rancho Mirage" where "food is more than just a meal—its a way of bringing people together."

**Cuisine Type:**
Spanish Mediterranean, blending "authentic flavors of Spain, Italy, France and Greece" with specialties including paella, charred Spanish octopus, charcuterie boards featuring Iberico Ham de Bellota, seafood, pasta, and meat dishes.

content.content:
# Catalan Mediterranean Restaurant - Summary

**Total Reviews:** 350 reviews

**Ranking:** #5 of 80 restaurants in Rancho Mirage

**Recent Sentiment (2025-2026):** Mixed. Recent positive reviews praise the food quality and atmosphere under new management, while negative reviews cite disappointment with paella portions, quality, and service inconsistencies.

**Common Praise:**
- Octopus appetizer consistently highlighted as "outstanding"
- Paella varieties and seafood dishes receive strong commendation
- New ownership (late 2024) credited with improvements in service and overall experience
- Elegant courtyard dining atmosphere

**Common Complaints:**
- Recent paella reviews mention inadequate portions for the $49 price point
- One reviewer noted paella used "cheap smoked paprika" instead of saffron
- Service inconsistencies reported, including slow staff responsiveness
- High noise levels in main dining area

**Awards:** Travelers' Choice award (mentioned as earned by restaurants in top 10% of Tripadvisor properties)

**Additional Details:** Restaurant features Spanish-Mediterranean cuisine, operates dinner service 5:00 PM-9:00 PM daily, offers full bar with craft cocktails, accepts reservations, and provides free parking.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best Spanish restaurants in the Coachella Valley and Palm Springs area that serve paella:

## Sol Y Sombra (Cathedral City)

Sol Y Sombra in Cathedral City is a vibrant haven for tapas lovers, celebrating the rich flavors of the Coachella Valley, nestled inside The NEW Paloma Resort's historic Adobe with an authentic Spanish-inspired menu using locally-grown ingredients like citrus, dates, and chilies. The paella is a standout dish, highly praised for its "phenomenal taste" and variety, ranging from traditional to vegetarian.

## Catalan Mediterranean Cuisine (Rancho Mirage)

Catalan Mediterranean Cuisine in Rancho Mirage is celebrated for authentic seafood offerings, with the restaurant's paella infused with traditional Spanish flair, bringing vibrant flavors of the sea to the desert, with diners praising it as "really flavorful" and "a must-try." The ambiance is enhanced by live Spanish guitar, creating an immersive dining experience.

## Alana (Palm Springs)

Alana is a culinary oasis in Palm Springs where vibrant flavors and handcrafted cocktails await, offering a unique experience with Spanish-inspired small plates and innovative mixology.

All of these restaurants offer excellent Spanish cuisine with notable paella selections in the Coachella Valley region.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Catalan in Rancho Mirage:

**Opening and Ownership:**
Catalan in Rancho Mirage opened in 2012 by father and son Mark and Drew Davis.

**Chef Drew Davis Background:**
Drew Davis was born in Los Angeles and knew at a young age that he had a true passion for food. He took a position in the catering department at Dean & Deluca in Kansas City and then moved on to New World Bistro, which was awarded Kansas City's Best New Restaurant of 1999 after his arrival in the kitchen.

Not satisfied with this early achievement, Drew worked tirelessly on learning every possible aspect of the restaurant business attending the Culinary Institute of America and working in Florida and the Mid-West. Drew moves to Miami in 2002 to train under Top Chef and James Beard award winners, Jonathan Eisman of Pacific Time and Chef Mark Militello of Mark South Beach.

Over the course of 9 years, Drew works his way up to become the Executive Sous-Chef at the prominent East Hampton, N.Y. powerhouse Nick and Toni's restaurant. Drew had worked in New York and moved to Los Angeles to be a private chef to Steven Spielberg before finally deciding to open a restaurant in the Palm Springs area.

**Restaurant Concept:**
Catalan is a Mediterranean restaurant, emphasizing on Italian and Spanish influences.

# Analysis of Catalan Mediterranean Cuisine Website

## Structured Data
**No schema.org markup detected.** The page lacks JSON-LD, microdata, or Restaurant schema. This is a significant SEO oversight—hours, address, and menu information are presented as plain text only.

## CMS Platform
Built on **GoDaddy Website Builder** (evident from footer attribution and URL structure patterns).

## Contact & Reservation Info
- Phone: (760) 770-9508
- Location: Rancho Mirage (mentioned as "heart of rancho Mirage")
- No street address provided in visible content

## Digital Features Present
- **Social media links**: Facebook, Instagram, Yelp
- **Reservation system**: Phone-based (no embedded widget visible)
- **Online menus**: Dinner menu, happy hour menu, kids menu linked
- **No online ordering** detected

## Recent Activity Indicators
- Happy hour promotion displayed
- Bar menu highlights featured
- "$49 Restaurant Week Menu" suggests recent/seasonal updates

## Key Gaps
The site lacks structured data markup, complete address information, and modern conveniences like table reservation widgets or online ordering—typical limitations of basic website builder platforms.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Catalan in Rancho Mirage and Palm Springs Life awards:

## Catalan Restaurant

Catalan Mediterranean Restaurant is located in the heart of Rancho Mirage and serves a variety of Spanish-infused dishes in a rustic yet modern setting. Chef Drew Davis opened Catalan Mediterranean Cuisine, and eight years later, it has become a desert staple, establishing itself as one of the Coachella Valley's most reputable, award-winning restaurants.

## Awards and Recognition

Catalan is described as a refreshing find, offering Mediterranean fare with farm-to-table sensibilities. On Tripadvisor, Catalan Mediterranean Restaurant is rated 4.3 of 5 and ranked #7 of 68 restaurants in Rancho Mirage.

The search results did not contain specific information about Catalan receiving a Palm Springs Life "Best Of" award, though the results show that Palm Springs Life does regularly honor businesses and establishments in the Coachella Valley area with various awards and recognitions.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best fine dining restaurants in Rancho Mirage for a date night:

## Top Recommendations

**The Edge Steakhouse**
The Edge Steakhouse at The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage is a world-class steakhouse created by award-winning Chef Darryl Boyd that serves American Wagyu cuts, all of which are dry-aged in house. It's a top choice for a romantic date night, with guests praising the panoramic sunset views, intimate dining room and attentive, celebratory service ideal for anniversaries and special evenings. This restaurant sits 650 feet above the valley, offering sweeping views of the adjacent mountain range.

**Wally's Desert Turtle**
Founded in 1978 by acclaimed restaurateur Wally Botello, Wally's Desert Turtle is revered as the origin of fine dining in the desert, offering a five-star experience. For date night, you can enjoy a meal in one of the cozy booths to experience the restaurant's candlelit ambiance, twinkling chandeliers and hand-painted murals.

**Willie's Modern Fare**
Willie's Modern Fare blends classic sophistication with a fresh, contemporary twist, serving artfully plated dishes that balance traditional European technique with vibrant California flavors including seared scallops with citrus beurre blanc and slow-roasted meats.

**Other Notable Options**
Shame on the Moon has been a fine-dining date night hot spot since its doors opened in 1984, with intimate, ornate and elegant dual dining rooms.

I found information about excellent restaurants in Rancho Mirage for you. Here are some top options:

## Fine Dining Options

Wally's Desert Turtle is a Rancho Mirage institution that delivers timeless elegance with refined California cuisine and a glamorous setting with mirrored walls, soft lighting, and crystal accents. This fine dining establishment has been a Palm Spring icon for more than 44 years. Nightly live musical entertainment is offered in the Sahara Lounge.

The Edge Steakhouse at The Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage has floor-to-ceiling windows that offer views of the sprawling desert landscape and signature purple-spun sunset. From prime cuts of beef cooked to perfection to a curated selection of decadent sides, the restaurant features impeccable service and a wine list featuring the finest vintages.

Willie's Modern Fare offers classic cuisine with a modern twist, complemented by inventive cocktails and a thoughtfully curated wine list.

## Other Notable Restaurants

Si Bon Belgian Bistro is a minimalist bistro that emphasizes its excellent French-Belgian menu, where diners can enjoy European flavors at breakfast, lunch and dinner.

Las Casuelas Nuevas is located at 70050 Highway 111 and has served great Mexican food in the Coachella Valley for over 40 years. It is the proud winner of Best of the Valley awards for Best Mexican Restaurant, Best Outdoor (Patio) Dining, and Best Sunday Brunch.

Kobe Japanese Steakhouse, founded in 1978, offers highly trained teppan chefs serving the finest ingredients in a warm and entertaining environment.

Catalan, nestled on HWY 111 in Rancho Mirage, specializes in Spanish Mediterranean cuisine bringing forth tastes from Italy, Spain, France and Greece.

Based on the search results, here are some excellent tapas restaurants near Palm Springs that feature Iberico ham and octopus:

## Top Recommendations

**Catalan Spanish Mediterranean Cuisine** (Rancho Mirage)
This restaurant features charred Spanish octopus and carries imported meats for their charcuterie boards including Iberico Ham de Bellota. It's a haven for seafood aficionados, with deep-water octopus often described as "tender" and "perfectly charred".

**Del Rey at Villa Royale** (Palm Springs)
Del Rey offers authentic Mediterranean dining with Spanish and Mediterranean-inspired tapas. Diners enjoy octopus dishes, and the restaurant features Spanish-style small plates on their menu.

**Sol Y Sombra** (Cathedral City)
Sol Y Sombra is a vibrant haven for tapas lovers. The paella is highly praised for its variety, ranging from traditional to vegetarian.

**Alana Bar + Tapas** (Palm Springs)
The tapas menu includes selections such as ceviche tostadas with octopus, shrimp, and white fish.

The Catalan Spanish Mediterranean Cuisine appears to be the best match for your specific interests, as it explicitly features both Iberico ham and charred Spanish octopus on their menu.

reputation.review_volume: Yelp ~999 reviews + 1,162 photos; TripAdvisor ~350 reviews; Google Maps cited as 300+ positive reviews (combined ~1,500+ across platforms)
reputation.sentiment: mostly positive (4.3/5 on TripAdvisor) with mixed recent signals
reputation.longevity: Opened 2012 by father-son team Mark and Drew Davis; ~14 years operating. Late-2024 ownership/management change referenced in recent reviews [UNVERIFIED specifics of new owner identity]
credentials: TripAdvisor Travelers' Choice (referenced in TripAdvisor profile)
Featured in Palm Springs Life (2013 chef profile)
Featured in Coachella Magazine, Locale Magazine, Coachella Valley Weekly

Chef Drew Davis trained under James Beard-winning chefs Jonathan Eisman and Mark Militello in Miami; CIA alum; former Exec Sous-Chef at Nick & Toni's (East Hampton); former private chef to Steven Spielberg
reputation.recency_signal: Recent reviews (2025-2026) note new ownership/management improving service and food; some complaints about $49 paella portions, saffron substitution, service inconsistency, and noise levels in main dining room. Sentiment leaning positive but with credibility caveats during transition.

agent_visibility.appears_in_queries: Surfaces strongly for: 'best Spanish restaurant Coachella Valley/Palm Springs', 'tapas restaurant near Palm Springs Iberico ham octopus', 'best paella Coachella Valley', 'Spanish restaurants Rancho Mirage'. Surfaces weakly or not at all for: 'date night Rancho Mirage' (loses to The Edge Steakhouse, Wally's Desert Turtle, Shame on the Moon, Willie's Modern Fare), 'best fine dining Rancho Mirage' (omitted from Palm Springs Life and OpenTable curated date-night lists), and generic 'best restaurants Rancho Mirage' top-10 mentions (typically a lower-third mention rather than headline).
agent_visibility.description_accuracy: Mostly accurate when surfaced - LLMs consistently identify it as Spanish-Mediterranean with paella, octopus, Iberico, sangria, and tapas. Chef-owner narrative occasionally gets garbled (some sources still attribute current operation to Drew Davis despite the late-2024 ownership transition). Cuisine framing as 'Mediterranean blending Spain/Italy/France/Greece' is faithfully echoed.
agent_visibility.site_structured_data: Weak. catalanrestaurants.com is built on GoDaddy Website Builder. NO schema.org / JSON-LD / Restaurant markup detected. Street address is not in visible body content (phone only). Hours are presented as plain text. Menu pages exist but are not semantically marked. Social links to Facebook/Instagram/Yelp present; no OpenTable widget embedded; no online ordering. A second domain (catalanrestaurant.com) hosts an About page, suggesting possible domain split/duplication post-ownership-change.

competitors: Sol Y Sombra (Cathedral City - Spanish tapas at The Paloma Resort)
Alana Bar + Tapas (Palm Springs - Spanish-inspired tapas)
Del Rey at Villa Royale (Palm Springs - Mediterranean/Spanish)
relationships.corridor_role: supporting anchor on the Rancho Mirage Highway 111 restaurant row. It is a category-defining independent for Spanish/Mediterranean cuisine in the mid-valley but not a top-tier date-night anchor in Rancho Mirage proper - that role belongs to Wally's Desert Turtle and The Edge Steakhouse (Ritz-Carlton). Catalan is the highest-profile independent Spanish concept on Highway 111 between Cathedral City's Sol Y Sombra and Palm Desert's Spanish/Med options.
query_wins: Iberico ham + octopus near Palm Springs

convertibility.ownership_signal: independent (locally owned, family-operated; opened 2012 by Mark and Drew Davis; appears to have transitioned to new local ownership/management in late 2024 per reviewer accounts) [UNVERIFIED identity of current owner]
convertibility.marketing_spend_signal: Mixed but present: paid OpenTable presence with reservation flow, active Yelp business profile (1,162 photos suggests owner-uploaded content), Facebook page (Catalan Rancho Mirage), Grubhub listing, Giftly gift-card integration, Restaurant Week participation ($49 menu), Desert Business Association membership, and consistent placement in local press features (Coachella Magazine, Locale, Coachella Valley Weekly). Real owned website (basic GoDaddy build) signals modest in-house effort rather than agency. No evidence of paid SEO/PR agency footprint.
convertibility.reasoning: Independently owned, locally operated, with a reachable decision-maker (single Rancho Mirage phone line, family-scale operation) and clear evidence of marketing intent across paid reservation platforms, social, and local-press placements. The GoDaddy-built site with no schema.org markup, missing street address in body content, and a possible parallel domain (catalanrestaurant.com vs catalanrestaurants.com) is exactly the profile of an independent that has marketing budget but is leaving agent-visibility value on the table. High convertibility for an AICV-style structured-data and category-mapping engagement.

sources_consulted: https://www.yelp.com/biz/catalan-mediterranean-cuisine-rancho-mirage-2 (403 on direct fetch; metadata via search)
flags: [UNVERIFIED] Exact identity and timing of the late-2024 ownership/management transition - referenced by multiple TripAdvisor reviewers but not confirmed by a primary source.
[UNVERIFIED] Whether Chef Drew Davis remains involved post-ownership-change - the about page at catalanrestaurant.com still narrates his backstory.
[UNVERIFIED] Specific Palm Springs Life 'Best Of' award - Drew Davis was profiled in PSL (2013) but no specific 'Best Of the Valley' or 'Restaurant of the Year' award was confirmed.

[UNVERIFIED] Whether catalanrestaurants.com and catalanrestaurant.com are operated by the same entity or represent a domain split - both appear in search results with overlapping but not identical content.
[UNVERIFIED] Google review count - cited as '300+ positive' by one third-party source but not directly verified from Google Business Profile.
[UNVERIFIED] No James Beard, Michelin, or Food Network credential found - chef trained under Beard-winning mentors but holds no personal Beard/Michelin recognition.

content.text: Assessment complete. Catalan Mediterranean Cuisine is a convertible independent on Highway 111 with strong category authority for Spanish/paella/tapas queries, a celebrity-trained chef pedigree, ~1,500+ reviews across platforms, but a weak technical web footprint (GoDaddy build, zero schema.org markup, missing address in body content, possible duplicate domain) and notable absence from Rancho Mirage date-night and fine-dining curated lists where Wally's Desert Turtle and The Edge Steakhouse dominate. Visibility gap scored 6/10 - real reputation, real marketing intent, real recoverable surface area.
